title: Giant Iceberg Breaks Free After 30 Years
story: A colossal chunk of ice the size of a small country started drifting again in 2023. Named A23a, this massive iceberg had been stuck in the Weddell Sea for over three decades. On November 24, scientists spotted it on the move once more. At about 1,500 square miles, it's the world's largest iceberg - imagine an ice cube as big as a city floating in the ocean!

follow_up_question: How long had the iceberg been stuck before it started moving again?

title: Stargazers Spot Venus Crossing the Sun
story: Two English astronomers made a groundbreaking discovery in 1639. Jeremiah Horrocks and William Crabtree became the first to observe Venus passing in front of the Sun. This rare event, called a transit, happened on November 24. Using methods from the famous astronomer Johannes Kepler, they predicted when Venus would cross the Sun's face. Their observation helped scientists better understand how big our solar system really is.

story: Guitar riffs and paintbrushes! John Squire, born in 1962 on November 24, isn't just a rock star. This talented musician from Altrincham, England, rocks out with bands like Stone Roses and The Seahorses. But when he's not strumming hit songs, Squire swaps his guitar for a canvas, creating colorful paintings that are as cool as his music.

title: Rocking with Huey Lewis & The News
story: Guitar strings hummed as Chris Hayes strummed his first chord. Born in Sacramento, California in 1957, Hayes would grow up to become a rock star. On November 24, he entered the world, destined for musical greatness. Hayes joined Huey Lewis & The News in 1980, helping create hit songs that got toes tapping across America.
activity_question: What band did Chris Hayes join in 1980?
bonus_fact: Chris Hayes played guitar on the popular song 'I Want A New Drug' by Huey Lewis & The News.

title: A Musical Maestro Is Born
story: Plucking strings and blending genres, Edgar Meyer entered the world on November 24, 1960. Born in Oak Ridge, Tennessee, this musical prodigy would grow up to become a renowned bassist and composer. Meyer's talent spans classical, jazz, and bluegrass, creating a unique sound that bridges musical worlds.

title: A Filmmaker's Punk Rock Surprise
story: Lights, camera, action... and punk rock? Emir Kusturica, born in Sarajevo on November 24, 1954, grew up to become a famous filmmaker. But here's the twist - he's also a punk rock bassist! Kusturica directed award-winning movies like 'When Father Was Away on Business' and 'Black Cat, White Cat.' His band? Fittingly named 'No Smoking.'
